ui.lede: Visual, verified walkthroughs of ownCloud Infinite Scale Web. Every step is a real end-to-end test, captured, not mocked.
ui.sourceNote: Generated from {source} and re-captured on every full CI run.
ui.footerLede: Every walkthrough is generated from a passing end-to-end test and re-captured on each full CI run, so the screenshots can never drift from the real product.
ui.footerMeta: Captured against ownCloud Infinite Scale {ocis}, Web UI {web}.
ui.loadError: Could not load content. Serve this folder over HTTP.

search.title: Searching your files
search.summary: Find files by name or by what is inside them, then narrow the results with filters.

steps.title: Start from the search bar
steps.caption: Wherever you are in ownCloud Web, the search bar sits at the very top. Click it and begin typing what you are looking for.

steps.title: Results appear as you type
steps.caption: Matches surface instantly in a dropdown. Pick one to jump straight to the file, or press <kbd>Enter</kbd> to open the full results page.

steps.title: It searches inside your files too
steps.caption: Results match on the file name and the file contents. Here annual-summary.txt matches because the word report appears inside it, shown highlighted.

steps.title: Narrow by when it changed
steps.caption: Open the Last modified filter to limit results to today, the last 7 or 30 days, this year, or last year.

steps.title: Match the file name only
steps.caption: Switch from Full text search to Title only to match file names alone. Content-only matches like annual-summary.txt drop away, leaving just budget-report.txt.

sharing.title: Sharing a file with a colleague
sharing.summary: Give someone in your organisation access to a file, choose what they can do, and keep track of it.

steps.title: Open the share panel
steps.caption: Hover a file and click the Share icon. The sharing panel opens on the right, ready to add people.

steps.title: Find your colleague
steps.caption: Type a name or email in the Search box. Matching people in your organisation appear, so pick the right one.

steps.title: Choose what they can do
steps.caption: Set the permission: <mark>Can view</mark> lets them view and download, while <mark>Can edit</mark> also lets them upload and change the file.

steps.title: Send the invite
steps.caption: Click Share. Your colleague now appears under Shared with with exactly the access you chose.

steps.title: Keep track of what you shared
steps.caption: Everything you share lives under Shares, Shared with others, where you can adjust the role or revoke access at any time.

spaces.title: Creating a project Space
spaces.summary: Set up a shared Space for a team or project, with its own storage and members, separate from your personal files.

steps.title: Start a new Space
steps.caption: Go to Spaces and click New Space. Give it a clear name, here Project Apollo, then create it.

steps.title: Your Space is ready
steps.caption: The new Space appears in your Spaces overview with its own storage quota, independent of your personal files.

steps.title: Open the members panel
steps.caption: Click Show members on the Space to manage who belongs to it. You start out as the Manager.

steps.title: Invite your team
steps.caption: Search for a colleague, pick a role, and click Add. Here Marie joins Project Apollo with Can view access.

tags.title: Organising files with tags
tags.summary: Label files with keywords so you can group and find them later, across folders and Spaces.

steps.title: Open a file's details
steps.caption: Select a file and open the details panel on the right. Among the file details you will find a Tags field.

steps.title: Add a tag
steps.caption: Type a label, like <mark>quarterly</mark>, and press <kbd>Enter</kbd>. Reuse an existing tag or create a new one on the fly.

steps.title: Tagged and findable
steps.caption: The tag sticks to the file. Later you can pull up everything with that label by searching tag:quarterly.

trash.title: Restoring a deleted file
trash.summary: Deleted something by mistake? Files are not gone immediately, so you can recover them from Deleted files.

steps.title: Find your deleted files
steps.caption: Open Deleted files from the left sidebar. Removed items wait here instead of disappearing for good.

steps.title: Select and restore
steps.caption: Tick the file you want back and click <mark>Restore</mark>.

steps.title: It is back
steps.caption: The file returns to its original location in your Personal space, exactly where it was before.

publiclinks.title: Sharing with a public link
publiclinks.summary: Create a link anyone can open, choose what they can do, protect it with a password, and manage it whenever you like.

steps.title: Pick the file to share
steps.caption: Open Personal and find the file you want to hand out. A public link works for a single file or for a whole folder.

steps.title: Open the Public links section
steps.caption: Hover the file and click the Share icon, then scroll to Public links in the panel and click Add link.

steps.title: Set the link options
steps.caption: Choose the access level, set a password (required here, and one is generated for you), and optionally add an expiry date.

steps.title: Choose what recipients can do
steps.caption: <mark>Can view</mark> lets people view and download. <mark>Can edit</mark> also lets them upload, change and delete, turning the link into a drop-off point.

steps.title: Copy and share the link
steps.caption: Click Copy link. ownCloud confirms it is on your clipboard, ready to paste into an email or chat, and the link now appears under Public links.

steps.title: Manage the link anytime
steps.caption: Open More options to rename the link, change its password or expiry date, or delete it to revoke access right away.

aidocsummary.title: Summarising a document with AI
aidocsummary.summary: Turn a long PDF, text or Markdown file into a short overview and a few key points, generated by an AI assistant right inside ownCloud. Preview of the AI Document Summary extension (web-extensions PR #452).

steps.title: Start from the file menu
steps.caption: Open a document's context menu. For supported files (PDF, TXT and Markdown) a Summarize action with a sparkle icon appears; it stays hidden for file types that cannot be summarised.

steps.title: Read the overview and key points
steps.caption: The Summary panel opens on the right with a 2 to 3 sentence overview and a handful of key takeaways. Use Regenerate to run it again.

steps.title: Summarise any supported document
steps.caption: It works the same for plain text and Markdown notes, not just PDFs, so you can grasp the gist of a file before you open it.

appstore.title: Discovering apps
appstore.summary: Browse, search and filter the apps available for your ownCloud from the built-in App Store.

steps.title: Open the App Store
steps.caption: Open the App Store from the application menu (the grid icon, top left). Every app available for your instance is shown as a card.

steps.title: Search by name
steps.caption: Type into the search box to narrow the list. Searching <mark>draw</mark> brings up the Draw.io diagram editor.

steps.title: Filter by category
steps.caption: Click a category tag such as viewer to show only apps of that kind.

steps.title: Open an app
steps.caption: Select any app to read its full description, screenshots and available versions.

appstore-details.title: App details and versions
appstore-details.summary: Inspect an app's details and download a specific version from the App Store.

steps.title: Start in the App Store
steps.caption: From the App Store overview, pick the app you are interested in.

steps.title: Read the app details
steps.caption: The details page shows the description, metadata and the list of released versions.

steps.title: Download a specific version
steps.caption: Download any listed version, not just the latest, which is handy for pinning a known-good release.

admin-general.title: General administration settings
admin-general.summary: Find your instance information and customise the appearance, including the logo, from the admin area.

steps.title: Open General settings
steps.caption: As an administrator, open Administration Settings and select General. The Info section shows your edition and versions.

steps.title: Upload a custom logo
steps.caption: Under Appearance, upload your own logo to brand the web interface.

steps.title: Reset to default
steps.caption: Changed your mind? Reset the logo to the ownCloud default at any time.

admin-groups.title: Managing groups
admin-groups.summary: Create, rename and delete groups to organise users and grant access in bulk.

steps.title: Open Groups management
steps.caption: In Administration Settings, open Groups to see every group in a table.

steps.title: Create a group
steps.caption: Use + New group to add a group; it appears in the list straight away.

steps.title: Rename a group
steps.caption: Select a group and edit its display name from the right-hand panel.

steps.title: Saved
steps.caption: The new name shows in the groups table. Removing a group is just as quick from its row actions.

download.title: Downloading files and folders
download.summary: Get a copy of any file, folder or shared item onto your device.

steps.title: Download from the file list
steps.caption: Every row has a Download action, and folders are packaged as a .zip. The right-click menu exposes it too.

steps.title: Download from the viewer
steps.caption: Open a file in the viewer and use the download icon in the toolbar to save the version you are looking at.

steps.title: Download shared items
steps.caption: The same Download action is available for files and folders others have shared with you, under Shares.

admin-spaces.title: Administering Spaces
admin-spaces.summary: Create, edit, disable and inspect project Spaces from the admin settings.

steps.title: Open the Spaces management page
steps.caption: Under Administration Settings, select Spaces in the sidebar. The table lists every project Space with its Manager, Members, Remaining quota and last modified time.

steps.title: Create a new Space
steps.caption: Use the New Space button to add a project Space (for example team A). A Space was created successfully message confirms it, and the new Space appears at the top of the list.

steps.title: Rename and adjust quota
steps.caption: Tick a Space to reveal the Edit quota and Disable actions in the toolbar. Renaming, changing the subtitle and raising the quota (here to 50 GB) each return their own confirmation toast.

steps.title: Disable or delete a Space
steps.caption: With a Space selected, the toolbar switches to Enable and Delete. Disabling takes it out of use without removing data, shown by the was disabled successfully message.

steps.title: Review Space members
steps.caption: Open the Members sidebar panel to see who belongs to a Space, grouped by role: Can manage, Can edit with versions and trashbin and Can view. Use Filter members to search a long list.

admin-users.title: Managing users
admin-users.summary: Create a user, edit their account details and assign them to groups from the admin settings.

steps.title: Open the Users management page
steps.caption: Under Administration Settings, choose Users. The table shows each account with its User name, Email, Role and Login status, and the Filter controls narrow the list by group or role.

steps.title: Create a new user
steps.caption: Select New user and fill in the account details (for example max). A User was created successfully message confirms it, and the new account joins the list.

steps.title: Edit a user's details
steps.caption: Pick a user and open the Edit user panel to change their name, email, password, Role, Login and Personal quota. Each change is saved in place, shown by Changes saved.

steps.title: Assign the user to groups
steps.caption: In the same panel, use the Groups field to add the user to one or more groups, such as sales department and finance department. Each group appears as a chip you can remove with its × button.

appmenu.title: Switching apps with the application menu
appmenu.summary: Use the grid icon in the top left to move between ownCloud's apps, like Files and the text editor.

steps.title: Open the application menu
steps.caption: The grid icon in the very top left corner is the application menu. Pick an app from it to switch context, here it opens the text editor on a new file.

steps.title: Work in the chosen app
steps.caption: Each app fills the window. Type your content, for example <mark>Hello world</mark>, then save with the disk icon in the top toolbar.

steps.title: Back to Files
steps.caption: Open the grid menu again and choose Files to return, where your saved New file.txt is waiting in the list.

batchactions.title: Acting on many files at once
batchactions.summary: Select several files and folders together, then apply a single action to all of them.

steps.title: Select with checkboxes
steps.caption: Each row has a checkbox on the left, and the box in the Name header is a select-all toggle. Tick several folders to act on them together instead of one at a time.

steps.title: Find the shares
steps.caption: Open Shares and the Shared with me tab to see everything others have sent you, each with its own checkbox so you can pick a whole batch in one go.

steps.title: Apply one action to all
steps.caption: With the rows selected, a single action (here the sync control next to each share) is applied across the whole selection at once, so you do not repeat the click per item.

breadcrumb.title: Navigating with the breadcrumb trail
breadcrumb.summary: Use the path at the top of the file list to see where you are and jump back to any parent folder in one click.

steps.title: See your full path
steps.caption: As you open folder after folder, the breadcrumb at the top grows: Personal > parent > folder > 'single-double quotes' > "inner" double quote > sub-folder. It always shows exactly where you are.

steps.title: Jump to an ancestor
steps.caption: Click any earlier name in the trail to jump straight back to that folder, skipping all the levels in between. No need to press back repeatedly.

steps.title: Climb back to the top
steps.caption: A few clicks later you are back at parent, near the top of the tree. The three dots next to the current folder open a context menu with actions for it.

fileviewer.title: Previewing files
fileviewer.summary: Open images, video, audio and more right in your browser, without downloading them first.

steps.title: Spot files by their thumbnails
steps.caption: In the file list, images and media show a small thumbnail so you can recognise them at a glance. Click a file name to open it in the viewer.

steps.title: The file opens in the viewer
steps.caption: The image fills the screen with no download needed. A toolbar along the bottom lets you zoom in and out, rotate, and go fullscreen.

steps.title: Flip between files
steps.caption: Use the arrows in the toolbar to move through the folder, shown as 6 of 7. The Details panel on the right shows size, owner, versions and more.

steps.title: Watch video inline
steps.caption: Video files like .webm play right in the viewer with standard playback controls, so there is no need to save them first.

steps.title: Play audio too
steps.caption: Audio files such as .mp3 open with a built-in player. Press play to listen without leaving ownCloud.

journey.title: A day in ownCloud: share this week's meal plan
journey.summary: Follow Alice as she prepares a folder, shares it with the parents, and they pick it up from their own accounts.

steps.title: Start in your personal space
steps.caption: Alice signs in and lands in her Personal space, the private home for her own files. From here she can create folders and upload this week's plan.

steps.title: Prepare the folder
steps.caption: She gathers the files into a folder and opens the Details panel to confirm the owner, size and last change before sending it out.

steps.title: Share with the parents
steps.caption: Using the share icons in the row's Actions, Alice invites the parents so everyone gets the same up to date plan.

steps.title: Parents see it instantly
steps.caption: Brian opens Shares and finds the meal plan and files waiting under Shared with me, with who shared them and when.

steps.title: Tidy up afterwards
steps.caption: Once the week is over, Alice selects the leftovers and removes them with the batch action, keeping her Personal space clean.

movecopy.title: Copying and moving files
movecopy.summary: Make a copy of a file in another folder, or move it somewhere new, without re-uploading.

steps.title: Start in a folder of files
steps.caption: Open any folder that holds the files you want to reorganise. Here Personal > Duplicate contains a file and a subfolder, ready to copy or move.

steps.title: Pick what to copy or move
steps.caption: Select one item, or tick several to act on them at once. You can right-click a row for Copy or Cut, drag and drop onto a folder, or use keyboard shortcuts.

steps.title: Paste and the copy appears
steps.caption: After Copy, pick a destination in the location picker and confirm. The new copy lands beside the original with a numbered name, like duplicate (1).txt.

steps.title: Moving puts it in the new place
steps.caption: Moving with Cut (or by dragging onto a folder) removes the item from where it was and drops it into the destination, like Sub1 inside this folder.

pagination.title: Paging through large folders
pagination.summary: Set how many items appear per page and step through a long file list with the pager.

steps.title: Choose items per page
steps.caption: Use the settings gear at the top right to set the items per page, for example <mark>20</mark>. The list now fills one page and a pager appears at the bottom.

steps.title: Move to the next page
steps.caption: Click 2 in the pager to see the remaining items. The footer keeps the running total, here 26 items with 223 B in total.

steps.title: Show hidden files
steps.caption: Turning on Show hidden files adds dot files to the count, so the footer updates and the pages refill to include them.

steps.title: Fit everything on one page
steps.caption: Raise the items per page to <mark>500</mark> and the whole folder fits at once, so the pager disappears entirely.

rename.title: Renaming files and folders
rename.summary: Give any file or folder a clearer name, in your own space or in something shared with you.

steps.title: Find the file to rename
steps.caption: Open the folder that holds the item, here example.txt inside a folder shared with you. The small pencil icon beside the name starts a rename.

steps.title: Type the new name
steps.caption: Click the pencil (or pick Rename from the row's action menu), type the new name like renamedByBrian.txt, and confirm to apply it.

steps.title: The renamed file
steps.caption: The list refreshes with the new name in place, for example renamedByAlice.txt. The same Rename action works on folders too.

searchspace.title: Searching within a Space
searchspace.summary: Find files that live in a specific project Space, with results that show which Space each match belongs to.

steps.title: Open your project Space
steps.caption: Go to Spaces in the left sidebar and open a project Space, here the team Space, where your shared project files live.

steps.title: The files in that Space
steps.caption: Inside the Space you see its own folders and files, separate from your Personal area.

steps.title: Search across everything
steps.caption: Type into the search box at the top and press <kbd>Enter</kbd>. Each result lists the folder it sits in, so you can tell project files apart from your own.

steps.title: See which Space a result belongs to
steps.caption: A match that lives in a project Space is tagged with that Space's name, like the <mark>team</mark> badge here, confirming the file is scoped to that Space.

shortcuts.title: Creating and opening shortcuts
shortcuts.summary: Make shortcut files that jump straight to another file, a folder or an external web page.

steps.title: Start in your files
steps.caption: Open the Files app and use the New button to create a shortcut, just like you would create a folder or document.

steps.title: Shortcuts appear in the list
steps.caption: Each shortcut is a small .url file with an arrow badge, such as docs.url or companyNews.url. They can point at a file, a folder or an external address.

steps.title: Open an external link
steps.caption: Selecting a shortcut like companyNews.url and opening it sends you straight to its target web page, for example https://owncloud.com/news/.

steps.title: Open the target file
steps.caption: A shortcut that points at a file opens that file directly, here landing in the text editor showing notice.txt in the docs folder.

activity.title: Following activity on files and Spaces
activity.summary: See a timeline of who changed what, for a single file or a whole Space.

steps.title: Make a change
steps.caption: Everyday actions are recorded as you work. Here a folder has just been renamed, shown by the 0 seconds ago in the Modified column.

steps.title: Activity for a file
steps.caption: Select a file and open the Activities panel to read its history: it was renamed, updated, and added, each entry stamped with who did it and when.

steps.title: Activity for a Space
steps.caption: Open a Space such as team and the same Activities panel covers everything in it: members joining, files added, and the Space being shared.

downloadspace.title: Download a whole Space
downloadspace.summary: Save the entire contents of a project Space to your device as a single archive.

steps.title: Open the Space
steps.caption: Go to Spaces and open the project Space you want to download, such as team.

steps.title: Download the contents
steps.caption: Use the Space's action menu (the three dots next to the name) and choose Download. Everything is packaged together as a single .zip.

steps.title: Members can download too
steps.caption: Anyone added as a member sees the same Space and can download its contents the same way, so the whole team has a copy when they need one.

gdprexport.title: Exporting your data
gdprexport.summary: Request a personal data export under GDPR from your account settings and download it when it is ready.

steps.title: Open your account page
steps.caption: From the user menu, open My Account and scroll to the GDPR section at the bottom. The GDPR export row lets you request a personal data export according to §20 GDPR.

steps.title: Request a new export
steps.caption: Click Request new export. ownCloud prepares an archive of your personal data in the background, and a Download link appears once it is ready.

steps.title: Download the export
steps.caption: When the export is finished, use the Download action next to GDPR export to save the archive to your device.

language.title: Changing the language
language.summary: Pick the language you want for the web interface, and watch it switch instantly.

steps.title: Open your account page
steps.caption: From the user menu, open My Account. Under Preferences, the Language row shows the language the interface currently uses, here English.

steps.title: Pick a new language
steps.caption: Open the Language dropdown and choose a language, for example Deutsch - German. The whole interface switches right away, so the page title becomes Mein Konto.

steps.title: Change saved
steps.caption: A short confirmation (Einstellung gespeichert, meaning the setting was saved) appears, and your choice is remembered the next time you sign in.

notifications.title: Notification settings
notifications.summary: Read in-app notifications from the bell, clear them, and control whether you get notification mails.

steps.title: Check the notification bell
steps.caption: The bell icon in the top bar shows a red count when something needs your attention. Click it to read recent activity, like Alice Hansen shared share_to_group with you.

steps.title: Mark all as read
steps.caption: Use Mark all as read at the top of the panel to clear the list. The bell count disappears and the panel shows Nothing new.

steps.title: Control notification mails
steps.caption: On My Account, the Notifications preference (Receive notification mails) controls whether events are also emailed to you. Untick it to keep notifications in-app only.

spacefromselection.title: Create a Space from selected items
spacefromselection.summary: Turn a folder or a selection of files into a brand new project Space in one step.

steps.title: Start in your Personal area
steps.caption: Open Personal and find the files and folders you want to group together, for example resourceFolder and lorem.txt.

steps.title: Create Space from selection
steps.caption: Select the items, then right-click and choose Create Space from selection. A confirmation reading Space was created successfully appears.

steps.title: Your new Space, ready to use
steps.caption: Open the new project Space under Spaces and your selected items are already inside, ready to share and collaborate on.

spacelink.title: Creating a public link to a Space
spacelink.summary: Share a whole project Space (or a folder inside it) with people who have no account, protected by a password.

steps.title: Create the public link
steps.caption: In the Space's Members panel, use Add link under Public links and set a password. The new link appears with its role (here Can view), and a toast confirms it was copied to your clipboard.

steps.title: Confirm the link exists
steps.caption: The Space Details now reads This space has one member and 1 link. Open Members any time to rename the link, change its role, or remove it.

steps.title: What the recipient sees
steps.caption: Anyone you send the link to lands on This resource is password-protected. They type the password under Enter password for public link and select Continue.

steps.title: Browsing the shared Space
steps.caption: After unlocking, the recipient sees the Space contents on a clean Public link page, where they can browse and open files according to the role you granted.

spacememberexpiry.title: Give a member time-limited access
spacememberexpiry.summary: Set an expiration date so a Space member's access ends automatically on the day you choose.

steps.title: Add the member
steps.caption: Open the Space and add the person from the Members panel. A Share was added successfully message confirms they now have access.

steps.title: Set an expiration date
steps.caption: Use the calendar icon next to the member, for example <mark>Brian Murphy</mark>, to pick a date. Their access ends automatically when that day arrives.

steps.title: Remove it to make access permanent
steps.caption: Changed your mind? Clear the date the same way and the member keeps access with no end date, confirmed by Share successfully changed.

spacemembers.title: Managing the members of a Space
spacemembers.summary: Add people and groups to a project Space, choose what each one can do, and remove access when it is no longer needed.

steps.title: Open your project Space
steps.caption: Go to Spaces and open the Space you manage, here team. The header shows the current count, for example 1 member, before you invite anyone else.

steps.title: Add members
steps.caption: Invite people or groups to the Space. Each added member produces a Share was added successfully confirmation, and the count rises (here to 5 members).

steps.title: Choose each member's role
steps.caption: Open the Members panel to set roles. Pick Can view, Can edit with versions and trashbin, or Can manage from each member's dropdown. Changes confirm with Share successfully changed.

steps.title: Remove a member
steps.caption: Use the three-dot menu next to a member to revoke their access. A Share was removed successfully message confirms they can no longer reach the Space.

spacespagination.title: Page through a Space with many items
spacespagination.summary: Move between pages, show hidden files, and change how many items appear at once in a large Space.

steps.title: Jump between pages
steps.caption: When a Space holds more items than fit on one page, the pager at the bottom lets you step through them. The footer shows the full count, such as 112 items with 1 kB in total.

steps.title: Reveal hidden files
steps.caption: Turn on Show hidden files from the display settings to include dot-prefixed items in the count and the list.

steps.title: Show more per page
steps.caption: Open the display settings (the gear icon) and raise the items per page, for example to <mark>500</mark>. With everything on one page, the pager disappears entirely.

upload.title: Uploading files and folders
upload.summary: Add files and whole folders to your space, one at a time or many at once.

steps.title: Start in your space
steps.caption: Open Personal and you see an empty list. Use the Upload button, or simply drag files and folders onto the page to add them.

steps.title: Files land in the list
steps.caption: Uploaded files show up right away with their size and a Modified time. The drag-and-drop area accepts mixed selections of files and folders at once.

steps.title: Upload a whole folder
steps.caption: Folders keep their structure when you upload them. Open PARENT and you find its files and subfolders exactly as they were on your device.

steps.title: Upload many files at once
steps.caption: You can select dozens of files in one go. Each is uploaded and listed individually, so a bulk import becomes a tidy file list.

uploadresume.title: Pausing and resuming a large upload
uploadresume.summary: Pause a big upload and pick it back up later without starting over.

steps.title: An upload in progress
steps.caption: Pick Files from the Upload menu and the progress overlay appears in the corner, showing how much time is left with pause and cancel controls.

steps.title: Pause when you need to
steps.caption: Press the pause control and the transfer stops where it is. The progress bar holds its position, ready to continue, instead of throwing away what was already sent.

steps.title: Resume from where it stopped
steps.caption: Press the resume control and the upload carries on from the same point. Large files survive a pause without having to be re-sent from the beginning.

steps.title: Finished and in your files
steps.caption: Once it completes, the file appears in the list at its full size, here a <mark>1.1 GB</mark> upload, confirming the resumed transfer arrived intact.

viewmodes.title: Tiles and list views
viewmodes.summary: Switch the files list between the condensed list, table and tiles layouts to suit what you are doing.

steps.title: Start in the list view
steps.caption: By default the files area uses a list layout. The three view buttons in the top right (condensed list, table and tiles) let you change how resources are shown.

steps.title: Switch to tiles
steps.caption: Click the tiles button (the grid icon) to show each file and folder as a large thumbnail tile, like tile_folder here. This view is handy for browsing images and folders visually.

steps.title: Your choice sticks
steps.caption: Open a folder and the tiles layout stays selected, so everything inside tile_folder keeps showing as tiles until you switch back.

fulltextsearch.title: Searching inside file contents
fulltextsearch.summary: Full-text search looks inside your documents, so you can find a file by a word it contains, not just by its name.

steps.title: Search across everything
steps.caption: Type in the global search bar and keep the All files filter. With full-text search enabled, ownCloud looks inside file contents as well as at file names.

steps.title: Matches inside the content
steps.caption: Searching for a word like <mark>Cheers</mark> returns documents that contain that word, even when it is nowhere in the file name.

steps.title: Refine the results
steps.caption: Narrow a large result set with the filter chips, for example by a Tag, media type or last-modified date.

steps.title: Open a hit
steps.caption: Open any result straight from the list to jump to the file you were looking for.

cast.title: Casting to a device
cast.summary: Send a file to a cast device using the Cast action this extension adds to the file menu.

steps.title: Pick a file
steps.caption: Start from a file in your space, here an image named logo.jpeg in Personal.

steps.title: The Cast action
steps.caption: Right-click the file and you will find Cast at the top of the menu, added by this extension to send the file to a cast device such as a Chromecast.

drawio.title: Diagrams with draw.io
drawio.summary: Create and edit draw.io diagrams directly inside ownCloud and save them back to your files.

steps.title: Open the app menu
steps.caption: Open the application menu (top left) to start a new diagram with the draw.io editor.

steps.title: Build your diagram
steps.caption: The full draw.io editor opens in ownCloud. Drag shapes from the left palette onto the canvas and connect them to build your diagram.

steps.title: Save your work
steps.caption: Use Save in the top right to store the diagram back into your files, then Exit to return.

steps.title: Saved as a file
steps.caption: Your diagram appears in the file list as a <mark>New file.drawio</mark> file, ready to reopen and edit anytime.

externalsites.title: External sites
externalsites.summary: Reach websites your administrator pinned, either in a new tab or embedded inside ownCloud.

steps.title: Open the app menu
steps.caption: Configured sites show up in the application menu (top left), here as Owncloud External and Owncloud Embedded entries.

steps.title: Pick an embedded site
steps.caption: Select an entry like Owncloud Embedded to open that website inside ownCloud.

steps.title: Site shown inside ownCloud
steps.caption: An embedded site loads in a page within ownCloud (here the ownCloud documentation), so you stay in the same window. External entries instead open in a new browser tab.

jsonviewer.title: Viewing JSON files
jsonviewer.summary: Open a .json file in a formatted, collapsible viewer instead of reading raw text.

steps.title: Add a JSON file
steps.caption: Upload or create a .json file in your Personal space, here a file named jsonFile.json.

steps.title: Open in JSON Viewer
steps.caption: Right-click the file and choose Open in JSON Viewer, which the extension adds alongside Open in Text Editor.

steps.title: Read it formatted
steps.caption: The file opens with syntax highlighting and a clean indented layout, so nested keys and values are easy to follow instead of one long line of text.

progressbars.title: Upload progress bars
progressbars.summary: Choose the style of the progress bar shown while files upload, from your account settings.

steps.title: Open your account
steps.caption: Go to My Account from the user menu, where preferences and extension options live.

steps.title: Choose a progress bar
steps.caption: Under Extensions, set Global progress bar to a style like Nyan Cat progress bar to customize what you see while files upload.

unzip.title: Extracting ZIP archives
unzip.summary: Unpack a .zip archive right where it sits, without downloading it first.

steps.title: Add a ZIP archive
steps.caption: Upload a .zip archive to a folder, here a file named data.zip in your Personal space.

steps.title: Extract here
steps.caption: Right-click the archive and choose Extract here, the action this extension adds to the file menu.

steps.title: Files unpacked in place
steps.caption: The contents land in a new folder next to the archive, so you can open them straight away without leaving ownCloud.

advancedsearch.title: Advanced Search
advancedsearch.summary: Find files with a dedicated search app that combines filters, full-text content search, and reusable saved searches.

steps.title: Open Advanced Search
steps.caption: The Advanced Search app opens with one big search box plus a full filter form below, ready before you type anything.

steps.title: Run a search
steps.caption: Type a term like test and press Search. An active Search: test chip appears and the KQL Query field fills in automatically as name:*test*.

steps.title: Refine with filters
steps.caption: Narrow results with Type, Media Type and Tags, a Content full-text field, or Size and Modified ranges. Clear All resets everything in one click.

steps.title: Save and reuse a search
steps.caption: Once a search is active, Save Search appears in the top right. Saved entries live behind Saved Searches so you can rerun a complex query later without rebuilding it.

photogallery.title: Photo gallery
photogallery.summary: The Photos extension gathers every image across your spaces into one visual timeline, with calendar and map views.

steps.title: All your photos in one place
steps.caption: Open Photos from the application menu. Every image across your personal space and Spaces is collected into a single grid, grouped by day.

steps.title: Open a photo
steps.caption: Select any photo to view it full screen, step through the set with the arrows, and read its details such as date, camera and location.

steps.title: Calendar and map views
steps.caption: Use the controls top right to filter to EXIF only images or switch between the Calendar timeline and the Map.

photolightbox.title: Photo lightbox
photolightbox.summary: Open a photo full screen to see it large alongside its EXIF metadata, and jump straight to where it was taken.

steps.title: Pick a photo
steps.caption: In the Photos gallery, click any thumbnail to open it in the lightbox.

steps.title: Full screen with details
steps.caption: The lightbox shows the photo large with a counter (1 / 6) and arrows to move through the set. Below it, the details panel lists Date taken, Camera, Model, Location and file info pulled from the image's EXIF data.

photomap.title: Photos on a map
photomap.summary: Plot your geotagged photos on a world map to see where each one was taken.

steps.title: Photos carry their location
steps.caption: Photos taken on a phone or GPS camera store coordinates in their EXIF data, shown here as Location with a View on Map shortcut.

steps.title: See them on the map
steps.caption: Switch to the Map view and every geotagged photo is dropped as a pin on a world map. The header reads Photos (4 of 4 in view); pan and zoom to explore, and click a pin to open those photos.

chatwithfile.title: Chatting with a file
chatwithfile.summary: Open a chat sidebar next to a PDF, text or Markdown file to ask questions about it, or have an AI assistant rewrite it for you in place. Preview of the Chat with File extension (web-extensions PR #454).

steps.title: Open the Chat panel
steps.caption: For a PDF, text or Markdown file, open the Chat panel from the sidebar (or the Chat with file context action). The assistant reads the file so you can talk about it.

steps.title: Ask about the file
steps.caption: Type a question and press <kbd>Enter</kbd>. The assistant answers from the file's own content, here summarising the project's main risks.

steps.title: Request an edit
steps.caption: Switch to Edit mode (available for text and Markdown files) and describe a change. The assistant proposes the rewritten file and shows a diff of exactly what it would change.

steps.title: Apply or discard
steps.caption: Review the diff, then Apply to file to save the change in place, or Discard to keep the original untouched.

versionchangelog.title: AI version changelog
versionchangelog.summary: For a file kept across several versions, generate a plain-language summary of what changed in each revision, written by an AI assistant from the actual diff between versions. Released as v0.1.0 of the Version Changelog extension (web-extensions).

steps.title: Open the Changelog panel
steps.caption: Select a text or Markdown file that has more than one saved version, then open the Changelog panel from the file sidebar. Each version is listed with its date, newest first.

steps.title: Generate an entry
steps.caption: Next to a version, click Generate. The extension diffs that version against the following one and sends only the changed lines to the configured AI endpoint, never the whole file.

steps.title: Read what changed
steps.caption: A short summary appears under each version, describing the edits in plain language, here that a launch date was added and an attendee removed, so you can scan a file's history without opening every revision.

groupmanagement.title: Managing groups
groupmanagement.summary: Create groups and manage who belongs to them from one screen. The Group Management app lists every group with its member count, lets you create, rename and delete groups, and add or remove users from a group. Preview of the Group Management extension (web-extensions PR #463).

steps.title: Open Group Management
steps.caption: Open Group Management from the app menu. The left pane lists every group with its member count; selecting a group shows its members on the right.

steps.title: Create a group
steps.caption: Click Create group, enter a name and confirm. The new group is added to the list and selected, ready for you to add members.

steps.title: Add members
steps.caption: On a group, choose Add members and pick users from the searchable list. Each member can be removed again from the group's detail panel.

passwordfolder.title: Password protected folders
passwordfolder.summary: Lock a folder behind a password so only people who know it can open and share its contents.

steps.title: A locked folder in your list
steps.caption: A password protected folder appears in Personal as a .psec item with a lock badge, like folder1 and folder2. The hidden .PasswordProtectedFolders folder holds the actual contents.

steps.title: Enter the password
steps.caption: Opening the folder shows This resource is password-protected. Type the password and select Continue. A wrong password is rejected with Incorrect password.

steps.title: Folder unlocked
steps.caption: With the correct password the folder opens and you can browse its contents. Use New or Upload to add files inside the protected folder.

steps.title: Share the locked folder
steps.caption: Copy the folder's link to share it, and a confirmation toast reports the link has been copied to your clipboard. Recipients open the link and unlock it with the same password.

webtour.title: Getting around ownCloud Web
webtour.summary: A tour of the ownCloud Web interface for everyday use: where your files live, how to switch apps, search everything, and find your account, storage and appearance settings. Re-captured automatically from a live oCIS instance so it never drifts from the product.

steps.title: Your files
steps.caption: After signing in you land in Personal, your private space. The left sidebar switches between Personal, Shares, Spaces and Deleted files; the main area lists whatever is in the current location.

steps.title: Switch applications
steps.caption: The application switcher in the top-left corner jumps between Files and the other apps enabled for you, such as the text editor, admin settings or installed extensions.

steps.title: Search everything
steps.caption: The search bar at the top finds files by name across everything you can access, and by their content when full-text search is enabled. Filters let you narrow a search to a space or file type.

steps.title: Your account and storage
steps.caption: The account menu in the top-right shows how much storage you have used, opens your Preferences, and lets you log out.

steps.title: Preferences and appearance
steps.caption: Open Preferences to set your language, switch between light and dark mode, and review your account details.

storagetour.title: Your files, shares and spaces
storagetour.summary: The left sidebar is your map to everything you can reach: your private files, what others have shared with you, collaborative spaces, and a trash bin for recovering deleted items. Re-captured automatically from a live oCIS instance.

steps.title: Personal
steps.caption: Personal is your private space. Only you can see what is here until you choose to share it. Upload files, create folders and organise your own documents.

steps.title: Shared with you
steps.caption: Shares collects everything other people have shared with you. Depending on the instance you may need to accept a share before it appears, and you can decline ones you do not want.

steps.title: Spaces
steps.caption: Spaces are shared project areas with their own members and storage. Use them to collaborate with a team in a dedicated location, separate from your personal files.

steps.title: Deleted files
steps.caption: Deleted files is your trash bin. Restore something you removed by mistake, or permanently delete it to free up space.

filesidebar.title: File details, sharing and versions
filesidebar.summary: Select any file and the right sidebar shows everything about it: its details, who it is shared with, and its previous versions. This is where most day-to-day file actions happen. Re-captured automatically from a live oCIS instance.

steps.title: See the details
steps.caption: Select a file to open the right sidebar. Details shows its size, when it was last modified, the owner, sharing status, tags and how many versions it has.

steps.title: Share with people
steps.caption: The Shares panel lets you invite registered users by name, or create a public link that anyone can open, optionally protected with a password and an expiry date.

steps.title: Choose what they can do
steps.caption: Pick a role for each person you share with. Can view lets them view and download; Can edit also lets them upload and change the file. Folders and spaces offer further roles such as Uploader and Manager.

steps.title: Restore an earlier version
steps.caption: The Versions panel keeps previous copies of a file. Open it to download or restore an older version, which is handy when several people edit the same document.

contextualhelp.title: Help where you need it
contextualhelp.summary: ownCloud Web keeps help close at hand: small question-mark icons sit next to features that benefit from a little explanation, and clicking one shows guidance specific to what you are doing without leaving the page. Re-captured automatically from a live oCIS instance.

steps.title: Spot the help icons
steps.caption: Throughout the interface a small ? icon sits next to features that need a little explanation, such as beside Share with people and Public links in a file's sharing panel.

steps.title: Open contextual help
steps.caption: Click a help icon to read short, context-specific guidance right where you are, here explaining how sharing with people works, with a Read more link to the full documentation.
